text.title: Chunking Production Service — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules: fail-fast config validation, dispatch by strategy enum, metadata propagation, logged fallback, strategy_used in output.

rule.statement: ChunkingService MUST validate ChunkingConfig at construction time. If strategy=SEMANTIC and embedding_func is None, raise ValueError immediately, not at chunk() time.
rule.rationale: Mid-batch failures are expensive; configuration errors must surface before the run starts.

rule.statement: Strategy MUST be a closed enum (FIXED, SENTENCE, PARAGRAPH, SEMANTIC, RECURSIVE, MARKDOWN, HTML, CODE). Free-string strategy values are rejected.
rule.rationale: Open strings drift across call sites; closed enum makes dispatch deterministic and refactor-safe.

rule.statement: Every emitted chunk MUST carry the caller-supplied document metadata dict merged with the chunker's own metadata. The caller's keys take precedence on conflict.
rule.rationale: Lost source/page/tenant metadata makes hits unattributable; merge policy must be explicit.

rule.statement: The fallback word-split path MUST be reachable only through the exception handler. Every fallback MUST log {source, requested_strategy, exception, traceback} at WARNING level, and emitted chunks MUST carry strategy_used="fallback" with the original requested strategy retained as `requested_strategy`.
rule.rationale: Silent fallback hides parse errors; operators cannot distinguish primary from degraded chunks without explicit tagging.

rule.statement: Every chunk in the service envelope MUST carry `strategy_used` field (the actual strategy that produced it). For audit, the envelope MUST also carry `requested_strategy`.
rule.rationale: Without strategy_used, A/B analysis cannot attribute quality changes; without requested_strategy, fallback frequency is invisible.
